descriptionShort: Profile of 1970-71 Vancouver Canucks expansion team players and their careers after hockey.
descriptionLong: Article profiles former 1970-71 Vancouver Canucks expansion players and their post-hockey careers. Includes Charlie Hodge (scout for Pittsburgh Penguins), Dan Johnson (owns jewelry store in Ontario), Orland Kurtenbach (coach of Richmond Sockeyes in BCJHL, operates golf driving range and insurance business), and Wayne Maki (died 1974 from brain tumor, had his number 11 retired). The expansion team paid $6 million for 18 players who scored just 37 goals total the previous season.
